fullText.MalthaelLastRites|MalthaelLastRites|Hero: Apply a death sentence to an enemy Hero that, after 2 seconds, deals damage equal to 50% of their missing Health.Quest: Enemies killed between the application of Last Rites and within 1.5 seconds of it dealing damage permanently reduce its cooldown by 5 seconds, to a minimum of 20 seconds.

fullText.MalthaelSoulRip|MalthaelSoulRip|Q: Extract the souls of nearby enemies afflicted by <c val="#MalthaelTrait">Reaper's Mark</c>, dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage and healing Malthael for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">25</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> per target hit. fullText.MalthaelTormentedSouls|MalthaelTormented: Unleash a torrent of souls, continually applying <c val="#MalthaelTrait">Reaper's Mark</c> to nearby enemies for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds.<n/><n/>Tormented Souls resets the cooldown of Wraith Strike when activated, and while active, Wraith Strike costs no Mana its cooldown is reduced to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds.

description.Abathur: A unique Hero that can manipulate the battle from anywhere on the map.
description.Alarak: A combo Assassin that can move enemies around and punish mistakes.
description.Alexstrasza: A Healer who shares her Health with allies and can transform into a Dragon to empower her Abilities.
description.Amazon: An Assassin who specializes against Physical Attackers.
description.Ana: A burst Healer who uses precise skillshots to heal allies and can deny enemy healing.
description.Anduin: A ranged Healer with the ability to pluck an ally from danger.
description.Anubarak: A Tank who specializes against Mages.
description.Artanis: A tough Bruiser that deals high damage if the enemy team is unable to shut him down.
description.Arthas: A Tank who is proficient against melee Physical Attackers, slowing them.
description.Auriel: A Healer that excels when paired with a high-octane damage dealer.
description.Azmodan: A Summoner that can also deal high damage from far away.
description.Barbarian: A Bruiser that's great at capturing Mercenary camps and likes to dive into the enemy team.
description.Butcher: An Assassin who starts slow, but deals extreme damage after collecting enough Meat.
description.Chen: A potent Bruiser that wears downs enemies and is resistant to burst damage.
description.Cho: Head 1 of a 2 Headed Hero, Cho controls the body of Cho'gall.
description.Chromie: A long range, high burst damage Mage, who requires good timing and setup.
description.Crusader: A Tank who shines against Physical Attackers and can effectively clear groups of minions.
description.Deathwing: A Massive Unstoppable Dragon that can withstand punishment while dishing it out, but is unaffected by allied Abilities.
description.Deckard: A burst Healer who excels at preparing the battlefield before a fight and can cripple enemies with crowd control.
description.Dehaka: A Bruiser with a strong, global presence that can effectively clear groups of minions.
description.DemonHunter: A well-rounded Assassin that's mobile and can deal high sustained damage.
description.Diablo: A risky Tank who can devastate an enemy team with his "all-in" combo potential.
description.Dryad: A sustained Assassin who can wear down her enemies from afar with her poison.
description.DVa: A hard-to-kill Bruiser that can peel enemies away from her allies with high disruption.
description.FaerieDragon: A Healer who can teleport to allies, excelling on large battlegrounds.
description.Falstad: An Assassin that can fly large distances, excelling on large battlegrounds.
description.Fenix: A sustained damage Assassin with a regenerating Shield that enables him to efficiently duel his opponents.
description.Firebat: A ranged Tank that specializes in initiating fights and dealing area damage by spreading his flames.
description.Gall: Head 2 of a 2 Headed Hero, Gall is a powerful Mage stuck to Cho.
description.Garrosh: A frontline Tank that enters the fray, trying to isolate a single opponent for elimination.
description.Genji: An opportunistic, mobile Assassin who can tear apart a weak backline.
description.Greymane: A high damage Assassin that wears down enemies from afar and dives in for the kill.10 Armor (in Worgen Form)
description.Guldan: A sustained damage Mage who uses his health as fuel but can steal life from his enemies.
description.Hanzo: A ranged Assassin who specializes in scouting and taking out enemies from a distance.
description.Hogger: A chaotic Bruiser that deals heavy area damage and can create terrain.
description.Illidan: A mobile, sustained damage Assassin who pairs well with Heroes that can enable him.
description.Imperius: An all-in Bruiser that leads the charge with strong initiation and single target damage.
description.Jaina: A frost Mage that can slow the entire enemy team.
description.Junkrat: A mobile Assassin that bombards the enemy from afar, and sows chaos with traps and mines.
description.Kaelthas: A fire Mage that punishes enemies that stay close together.
description.KelThuzad: A burst damage Mage that locks down enemies and devastates them with powerful combos.
description.Kerrigan: A combo Assassin who goes all in, diving into the enemy team.
description.L90ETC: A Tank who supplies his team with a good bit of utility.
description.Leoric: A Bruiser that deals high damage against teams with many high Health Heroes.
description.LiLi: A sustained Healer who works well against Physical attackers by Blinding them.
description.LostVikings: Three Heroes in one! Can gather experience from multiple locations.
description.Lucio: A sustained Healer who gives his team mobility and peels enemies off allies.
description.Maiev: A mobile Assassin that excels against clustered enemies and prevents their escape.
description.Malfurion: A strong sustained Healer who can help lockdown enemies to secure takedowns.
description.MalGanis: A self-sustaining Tank that peels for allies and disrupts enemies.
description.Malthael: A Bruiser who marks his enemies, shredding away high Health enemies.
description.Medic: A strong, sustained Healer who's unbeatable if left alone by her enemies.
description.Medivh: A Support who can negate burst damage and allow his allies to teleport.
description.MeiOW: A mobile Tank who controls and disrupts the battlefield with weather-altering technology
description.Mephisto: A sustained Assassin who thrives in battle against groups of enemies.
description.Monk: A flexible Healer that pairs well with Melee allies who like to dive into the enemy team.
description.Muradin: A sturdy Tank that can do well in a multitude of situations.
description.Murky: Not quite a full Hero, Murky can place an Egg to respawn quickly back into battle.
description.Necromancer: A Summoner who can push multiple lanes with ease.
description.NexusHunter: A brutal Melee Assassin who is adept at both beginning and ending fights.
description.Nova: A burst damage Mage who can ambush enemies from Stealth.
description.Orphea: A ranged Assassin who dances around her enemies while using spells to empower her attacks.
description.Probius: A high damage Summoner that must stay near Pylons to be effective.
description.Ragnaros: A Bruiser that can nullify enemy pushes against his Forts and Keeps.
description.Raynor: A Physical Damage Assassin that can keep enemies away.
description.Rehgar: A well-rounded Healer that pairs well with Melee allies.
description.Rexxar: A Bruiser that with his bear, Misha, expertly controls areas of the battleground.
description.Samuro: A stealthed Assassin that's great at capturing Mercenary camps and is difficult to kill.
description.SgtHammer: A long-range, Physical Damage dealer that needs protection from allies.
description.Stitches: A Tank who wants to isolate distant enemies away from their team.
description.Stukov: A Healer that brings high amounts of disruption from long range.
description.Sylvanas: An Assassin who can possess enemy minions
description.Tassadar: A psychic Mage that can deliver area damage from long distances.
description.Thrall: A Bruiser who can hold his own in a lane of battle.
description.Tinker: A Summoner that specializes in controlling key areas of the battleground.
description.Tracer: A mobile Assassin who picks off straggling enemies, finishing them off.
description.Tychus: An Assassin who shreds High Health enemies.
description.Tyrael: A Tank who pairs well with allies that like to dive into the enemy team.<n/>
description.Tyrande: A Healer who helps her allies lockdown and finish off enemies.
description.Uther: A Healer that can negate high bursts of damage.
description.Valeera: A stealthed Assassin who specializes in shutting down her opponents.
description.Varian: A Bruiser that can flex into dealing damage or protecting his team.
description.Whitemane: A versatile Healer who heals her allies by smiting her foes.
description.WitchDoctor: A sustained damage Mage who gains power when killing enemy minions.
description.Wizard: A high burst damage Mage who can reset her Abilities upon getting a takedown.
description.Yrel: A deliberate melee Bruiser whose heavyweight abilities reward investment and careful timing.
description.Zagara: A Summoner who provides lots of vision of the battleground.
description.Zarya: A Support that can enable deadly allies and is good at preventing burst damage.
description.Zeratul: A stealthed Assassin who can dart around his enemies.
description.Zuljin: A Physical Damage Assassin that gains power while losing Health.

infoText.Abathur: Abathur, the Evolution Master of Kerrigan's Swarm, works ceaselessly to improve the zerg from the genetic level up. His hate for chaos and imperfection almost rivals his hatred of pronouns.
infoText.Alarak: Not all heroes are born of altruism... some, like Alarak, simply desire vengeance. As the new Highlord of the Tal'darim, Alarak leads his people to a destiny free of the corrupt influence of the fallen xel'naga, Amon.
infoText.Alexstrasza: Alexstrasza, the queen of dragons, was empowered by the titans to be the guardian of all life on Azeroth. She has aided mortals time and again, even at great personal cost. Now, she soars across the Nexus, protecting life wherever she finds it.
infoText.Amazon: After shattering the Worldstone, the young Amazon Cassia had changed. She had seen hatred, terror, and destruction firsthand. If the Askari were to survive the coming darkness, they needed an army. She would begin their training immediately.
infoText.Ana: As a founding member of Overwatch and one of the world's best snipers, Ana Amari uses her skills to protect the innocent. Though she went MIA after losing her eye, Ana's sense of duty and responsibility has brought her back to the fight.
infoText.Anduin: As a young man, Anduin sought peace as a priest of the Holy Light and an envoy of the Alliance. After his father, Varian, fell to the Burning Legion, Anduin took up the crown as High King of the Alliance. He soon learned that peace must be fought for, even in the Nexus.
infoText.Anubarak: Once a proud nerubian king, Anub'arak dared to stand against the Lich King's invasion, but despite his bravery, he too fell to the unrelenting Scourge. Resurrected into undeath, the Traitor King now serves as a towering weapon of destruction.
infoText.Artanis: Despite his relative youth, Artanis was named Hierarch of the Daelaam after the fall of Aiur. Driven by the promise of a brighter future, he hopes to reclaim the protoss' fallen home world and rebuild their once glorious civilization.<n/><n/>Artanis is FREE to purchasers of StarCraft® II: Legacy of the Void™
infoText.Arthas: Once the crown prince of Lordaeron, and protégé of Uther the Lightbringer, Arthas was corrupted by the cursed blade Frostmourne in a bid to save his people. Now he rules over the damned as the Lich King and all shall know his wrath.
infoText.Auriel: It is through the archangel Auriel that the power of hope flows into the fabric of creation. Her eternal light illuminates even the darkest souls. Seeking harmony in all things, she is a mediator, a counselor, and when the need arises, a fearless warrior.
infoText.Azmodan: The Lord of Sin rules over a densely populated land of Hell where both the greatest joys and despair, and the deepest depravities can be experienced. But it is never enough for Azmodan, who has set his eyes on the realm of Sanctuary.
infoText.Barbarian: From the frozen Dreadlands of the north come the mighty Barbarian warriors. After surviving the destruction of her home and death of her tribe, Sonya wanders Sanctuary in search of battle and a better life for her people.
infoText.Butcher: An unrelenting hacking echoed off the walls of the Cathedral and as Prince Aidan made his way through its depths, the sound only grew louder. There were no screams; just that dreadful sound... over and over.
infoText.Chen: To be a brewmaster is to be an explorer, a lover of nature, and when the need arises; a powerful fighter. For years Chen Stormstout has wandered the world of Azeroth in search of rare ingredients, stirring stories, and of course, good brew.
infoText.Cho: Cho’gall is one of the most powerful, intelligent, and insane ogre-magi to have ever lived. As the leader of the Twilight's Hammer the two brothers seek to bring about an age of destruction... whenever they aren’t bickering that is.
infoText.Chromie: At first glance, Chromie appears to be an ordinary gnome. In truth, she is Chronormu, Bronze Dragon and a master of time. When it comes to temporal manipulation there's no problem she can't solve... with some help from her future self, of course.
infoText.Crusader: There have been many women who have borne the name Johanna in service of the crusade, and each has given her life to purify her faith. Johanna carries on their legacy as she searches the kingdom of Khanduras for the fallen star.
infoText.Deathwing: One of the five Dragon Aspects and the leader of the Black Dragonflight, Neltharion the Earth-Warder was once a protector of Azeroth. During the War of the Ancients, he was driven mad by the Old Gods and turned against his former allies. Now known as Deathwing the Destroyer, he has found his way to the Nexus where his limitless rage drives his ultimate goal of widespread destruction and the eventual end of all living things.
infoText.Deckard: As an avid scholar, Deckard Cain has dedicated his life to the pursuit of knowledge. He has been an advisor, storyteller, and friend to Sanctuary's greatest heroes, guiding them through peril time and again. When Deckard speaks, it's wise to stay awhile and listen.
infoText.Dehaka: Cunning and wise, Dehaka is one of the last surviving pack leaders of the forgotten world of Zerus. As with all primal zerg, he is driven by a powerful desire to consume the essence of his foes and evolve into newer and more deadly forms.
infoText.DemonHunter: Valla witnessed the ferocity of hellspawn firsthand as the demons ravaged her village and left her for dead. Now, with no people to call her own, her only allegiance is to her cause: to rid Sanctuary of the demonic filth corrupting its lands.
infoText.Diablo: Known in the ancient tongue as Al'Diabolos, the Prime Evil of Terror, the Demon Lord Diablo seeks to claim the world of Sanctuary for the Burning Hells. His victims are innumerable, his schemes unknowable, and his gender... malleable.
infoText.Dryad: While her younger sisters frolicked in idyllic forests, Lunara watched as mortals exploited nature’s splendor. On Azeroth, her father Cenarius forbade her from retaliating. But this is the Nexus, and it’s time for nature to take its revenge.
infoText.DVa: Hana Song, also known as "D.Va," is both a world champion professional gamer and a burgeoning global icon. As a member of South Korea’s elite MEKA unit, she fights to protect her homeland from the colossal omnic threat lurking within the East China Sea.
infoText.FaerieDragon: Like many faerie dragons, Brightwing is happiest darting around the forests of Ashenvale, healing friends, and then grabbing a light snack. By the way, did you know a human head only weighs ten pounds? Talk about light!
infoText.Falstad: Falstad is the High Thane of the Wildhammer Clan and a founding member of the Council of Three Hammers that presides over Ironforge. Despite speculation, Falstad has never been dead and anyone who claims such is a liar.
infoText.Fenix: As one of the greatest heroes in protoss history, Fenix's deeds on the battlefield are legendary. After falling to the zerg during the invasion of Aiur, his body was recovered and implanted into a dragoon so he could continue to fight. Not even death can stop Fenix from achieving glory and valor.
infoText.Firebat: Cpl. Miles "Blaze" Lewis has always been drawn to the flame, but it wasn't until he joined up with Raynor's Raiders that he put his pyromania to good use. He's seen his fair share of battles since then, and has never failed to bring the heat.
infoText.Gall: Cho’gall is one of the most powerful, intelligent, and insane ogre-magi to have ever lived. As the leader of the Twilight's Hammer the two brothers seek to bring about an age of destruction... whenever they aren’t bickering that is.
infoText.Garrosh: As the new warchief of the Horde, Garrosh is a proud and vicious warrior who rules with an iron fist. His hatred for the Alliance burns like wildfire, and he will stop at nothing to destroy them. By his axe, the orcs will reclaim their rightful glory.
infoText.Genji: Once the carefree youngest scion of the Shimada clan, Genji was cut down by his own brother for refusing to take part in their illegal ventures. Grafted into a cyborg body by Overwatch, Genji walks his own path as one who fights for what's right.
infoText.Greymane: Like most Gilneans, Genn Greymane is afflicted by the worgen curse. Though he feels at fault for his people’s ill fate, he has embraced his inner beast without giving in to its feral nature. Now he leads his pack as both their alpha and their king.
infoText.Guldan: Abandoning the ways of shamanism, Gul'dan entered a pact with the demon lord Kil'jaeden and betrayed his people in exchange for limitless power. With the orcish Horde under his master's thrall, both Draenor and Azeroth nearly fell into his grasp.
infoText.Hanzo: Once heir to the Shimada clan's criminal empire, Hanzo abandoned his birthright after he almost killed his younger brother. He now strives to perfect his skills as a warrior, and sees the Nexus and its endless conflict as the ideal training ground.
infoText.Hogger: Once largely a threat to fledgling adventurers’ egos in Elwynn Forest, the Riverpaw gnoll Hogger has finally found a realm vast enough to sate his hunger… which is expanding to fill the space.
infoText.Illidan: Illidan Stormrage, the first demon hunter, betrayed his people and created a new Well of Eternity after the demonic invasion of Azeroth. Now Illidan lords over Outland slaying unsuitably prepared Heroes who venture into his domain.
infoText.Imperius: Leader of the Angiris Council and Aspect of Valor, Imperius has valiantly led the armies of the High Heavens to innumerous victories over the demons of the Burning Hells. While his methods are exact and severe, there is no greater defender of righteousness in the entirety of creation.
infoText.Jaina: Once the apprentice of the Archmage Antonidas, Jaina Proudmoore led the survivors of Lordaeron to Kalimdor and founded the city of Theramore. Now, she serves the Alliance as a powerful voice fighting for reason and diplomacy.
infoText.Junkrat: The destruction of the Australian omnium left the area an irradiated waste and survivors like Jamison Fawkes were greatly affected by radiation. Now as the explosive-obsessed criminal Junkrat, he is renowned for leaving havoc and bedlam in his wake.
infoText.Kaelthas: Like all Blood Elves, Prince Kael'thas was transformed by the fall of Quel'Thalas. Desperate to save his people from being consumed by their magical addiction, he joined forces with the Burning Legion and now lords over Tempest Keep.
infoText.KelThuzad: Kel'Thuzad has faithfully served the Lich King both in life and after death. Reanimated as the Archlich of the Scourge and given domain of the Dread Necropolis, Naxxramas, Kel’Thuzad remains Arthas’s most trusted advisor and master of the cold dark.
infoText.Kerrigan: Once a terran ghost with formidable psionic abilities, Sarah Kerrigan was betrayed by her allies and transformed into the Queen of Blades. Now freed of the dark one's corruption, Kerrigan faces a threat that could destroy the galaxy itself.
infoText.L90ETC: The Power of the Horde incarnated by brutal rhythms and blistering guitar riffs into the most elite tauren chieftain to ever headbang across the surface of Azeroth. Don't bother putting on socks. He's just going to rock them off anyways.
infoText.Leoric: Madness. Betrayal. Death. These are the legacy of the Black King Leoric's reign. Driven to madness by the spirit of Diablo, Leoric brought untold suffering on his kingdom. Even after his death there was no release for the Skeleton King.
infoText.LiLi: Born upon Shen-zin Su, the Wandering Isle, Li Li Stormstout led an uneventful life until she began reading her uncle Chen's journals. Soon, nothing would sate her desire for adventure, exploration, and pestering adventurers around Halfhill.
infoText.LostVikings: The Lost Vikings are back! While they briefly considered retirement after defeating the emperor Tomator, there were only so many polar bears they could wrestle before falling into a Nexus portal seemed like a solid life decision.
infoText.Lucio: From the streets of Rio to the clubs on King's Row, Lúcio's beats bring the party to life, and drive the people to action. Now he's on tour in the Nexus, ready to break it down, and to continue fighting for what's right.
infoText.Maiev: Maiev Shadowsong stood watch over the imprisoned Betrayer for ten thousand years, and hunted him relentlessly after he was released. Some say she's determined; others say she's obsessed. Either way, she will stop at nothing to ensure the security of her world.
infoText.Malfurion: Malfurion Stormrage is the greatest druid on Azeroth, and a student of the demi-god Cenarius. Communing with nature and Cenarius through the Emerald dream, Malfurion protects the wilds from demonic influences.
infoText.MalGanis: Cunning and intelligent, Mal'Ganis was chosen to bring Arthas Menethil into the Lich King’s service. After manipulating the prince into purging Stratholme of life, he finally fell to Frostmourne as Arthas's first act as the Lich King's champion... yet a demon does not die so easily.
infoText.Malthael: Once the Aspect of Wisdom, Malthael abandoned heaven after the Worldstone's destruction. In secret, he had resolved to end the Eternal Conflict by becoming a Reaper of Souls and eliminating everything affected by demonic corruption, including humanity.
infoText.Medic: To good marines, the only thing more important than the rifle in their hand is the medic at their back. As one of the first officers deployed during the United Earth Directorate's invasion, Lt. Rosa Morales is the best of the best.
infoText.Medivh: Under the demon lord Sargeras's influence, Medivh opened the Dark Portal and unleashed the Horde upon Azeroth. To atone for this sin, he rallied a new generation of heroes to stand against the Burning Legion. Now, he takes his place amongst legends.
infoText.MeiOW: A brilliant mind with a sunny disposition, Dr. Mei-Ling Zhou joined Overwatch to help solve the world's growing climate problems. Armed with a host of weather-altering technologies, Mei now fights to protect her friends and secure a future for a troubled world.
infoText.Mephisto: Dul’Mephistos, the Lord of Hatred, is said to be the most cunning among his brethren. Even while imprisoned in a soulstone, his vile influence corrupted the Zakarum and manipulated them into breaking the seal, giving him free reign to reunite the Prime Evils and enslave humanity.
infoText.Monk: To the monks of Ivgorod, the gods are in all things, and their divine will is proclaimed by the Patriarchs. With unshakable faith, Kharazim carries forth the decrees of the thousand and one gods to the ends of Sanctuary and the realms beyond.
infoText.Muradin: Once a mentor to Prince Arthas, Muradin was unable to prevent his corruption. After recovering from a mild case of amnesia, he is now the leader of the Bronzebeard clan after the "diamond-ization" of his brother King Magni.
infoText.Murky: Mrgglglbrlg rmrmgllg mrggggm. Mrrglglgy, mgllglgl mgggrrmgl? MRGGGLGLLM! Mrrggllgggllggll mrrglrlg mrrg mrrg mrrrg.
infoText.Necromancer: From the recesses of the eastern jungles comes a man cloaked in mystery. As a member of the secretive Priests of Rathma, or the Necromancers, Xul wields powerful death magic and fights to preserve the delicate balance between order and chaos.
infoText.NexusHunter: A sworn knight and holder of a singularity shard, Qhira's only desire is to seek out others who may have escaped the destruction of her doomed home realm, Iresia. While she searches, she gets by as a bounty hunter – a job she loathes, despite her exceptional skill.
infoText.Nova: November "Nova" Terra is a ghost of the Terran Dominion and one of the most powerful psionic soldiers ever known. Nova is highly focused, determined, and extremely deadly. Even Jim Raynor wouldn't want to be caught in her crosshairs.
infoText.Orphea: Rejecting the dark power her father had begun to consort with, Orphea tried to run away from the Raven Lord's influence. But after witnessing King's Crest fall to his machinations, she was convinced she needed to stand and fight. She now vows to protect the innocent--as a Hero of the Nexus.
infoText.Probius: Since his activation, Probius has always wanted to prove himself. He may be small, but he made a big difference by warping in a critical pylon during the retaking of Aiur. As the bravest of probes, Probius is eager to fulfill his purpose in the Nexus.
infoText.Ragnaros: As the lord of Azeroth’s fire elementals, Ragnaros desires nothing more than to burn the world to ash. Banished to the Firelands eons ago, the Firelord now turns his rage toward the Nexus, intent on incinerating everything and everyone within it.
infoText.Raynor: Ex-Confederate Marshal James Raynor has stood against whatever the universe can throw at him and survived. He stands as a beacon of hope among enigmatic aliens and monsters, fighting for justice in a cold uncaring universe.
infoText.Rehgar: Rehgar Earthfury has seen his share of combat, both as a gladiator, and a master of his own team. Now, having put that life aside, he serves as a simple shaman of the Earthen Ring, earning his redemption by healing the lands of Azeroth.
infoText.Rexxar: It was a strange twist of fate that led the wandering half-ogre Rexxar to the fledgling city of Orgrimmar. With his aid, Thrall and the Horde were able to secure their homeland and defend it from the invading forces of Lord Admiral Daelin Proudmoore.
infoText.Samuro: Samuro, the last remaining blademaster to wear the banner of the Burning Blade, is on a warrior's pilgrimage in search of vengeance and redemption for his fallen clan. It will be a long journey, but he has sworn an oath to restore his clan's honor.
infoText.SgtHammer: Sergeant Bama "the Hammer" Kowalski has served some of the toughest tours around the Koprulu sector. The roar of her siege tank's shock cannon strikes fear right into the heart of the toughest of warriors... including some of her allies.
infoText.Stitches: Take care when traversing the shadowed trails of Duskwood...<n/>Ever since Abercrombie the Embalmer unleashed his vile abomination upon Darkshire, Stitches has wandered the road aimlessly, hungering for the flesh of low level Alliance players.
infoText.Stukov: Alexei Stukov had a hard life, and an even harder death. He has been betrayed, shot into space, resurrected and infested, almost cured, and then experimented upon as the infestation returned. Now, his infestation spreads within the Nexus...
infoText.Sylvanas: Sylvanas Windrunner was not always the Dark Lady of the Forsaken. In another life, she was the Ranger-General of Silvermoon. But that was before the Death Knight Arthas slew her and her people and raised her as the first Banshee.
infoText.Tassadar: An Executor without equal, Tassadar has fought tirelessly to purify the Koprulu sector of zerg infestation. Only now, after learning to wield the power of the void, is Tassadar finally ready to face the Overmind and its all-consuming Swarm.
infoText.Thrall: As a child, Thrall served the cruel Aedelas Blackmoore as a slave. After gaining his freedom, he guided the liberated orcs to a land of their own and worked to recover their ancient traditions. Now and forever he is the Warchief of the Horde.
infoText.Tinker: Few would consider Gazlowe a fighter, but what he lacks in height, he makes up in mechanical know-how. Besides, you don't get to be boss of a "boom"-town like Ratchet with just a charming personality, if you know what I mean.
infoText.Tracer: Lena Oxton (call sign: "Tracer"), is both an adventurer and an irrepressible force for good. With the ability to alter her own time at will, she fights the good fight wherever she goes--even the Nexus. Don't worry, loves. The cavalry's finally here.<n/><n/>Tracer is FREE to purchasers of Overwatch™: Origins Edition
infoText.Tychus: Tychus Findlay is a big man with a big ego and a bigger gun. Fortunately, his loyalty is easily won. Buy him a drink or three and promise him enough credits, and there's no job in the whole universe too dangerous for this outlaw.
infoText.Tyrael: Among the angels it is the Archangel of Justice Tyrael who is humanity's greatest defender. Wielding his sword El'druin against the Burning Hells, the Prime Evils would have enslaved humanity long ago if Tyrael had not intervened.
infoText.Tyrande: Tyrande Whisperwind strikes fear into all who wish ill of the Kaldorei. As the highest ranking Sentinel and High Priestess of Elune, she is not only the night elves' most deadly warrior, but also their most cunning leader.
infoText.Uther: The first paladin and founder of the Order of the Silver Hand, Uther the Lightbringer is a faithful servant of the Light. Dispensing justice with the swing of his hammer, Uther is a bastion of truth and an unflinching symbol of the Alliance.
infoText.Valeera: For a time, the gladiator Valeera Sanguinar served dutifully as one of Varian Wrynn's personal bodyguards. Now, her shadowy skills find her a natural fit within the secretive rogue order, the Uncrowned, in their fight against the Legion.
infoText.Varian: Few would have expected that the gladiator known only as Lo'Gosh was in truth the missing ruler of Stormwind, Varian Wrynn. Now the High King of the Alliance, his every action shapes the destiny of Azeroth itself.
infoText.Whitemane: As the High Inquisitor of the Scarlet Crusade, Sally Whitemane zealously leads her champions in a neverending war against the undead. Under her guiding Light, all who have been touched by the plague of death will be purged by her cleansing flame... no matter how much they protest.
infoText.WitchDoctor: With cabalistic incantations and vestments of feather and bone, the witch doctor Nazeebo calls upon spirits from the Unformed Land to aid him. Few outlanders encounter a witch doctor in the flesh, without losing their own.
infoText.Wizard: The derogatory moniker "wizard" is often given to mages with rebellious attitudes or an obsession with power. Li-Ming displays a little of both and she's hardly ashamed of it. After all, who are they to judge a wielder of limitless arcane magic?
infoText.Yrel: After traveling to an Alternate Draenor, the forces of Azeroth saved the acolyte Yrel from the Iron Horde. With their aid, she rose to the rank of Exarch and fought back the demonic invasion of her world. Now, she finds herself where time, space, and dimensions converge: the Nexus.
infoText.Zagara: If Kerrigan is the Queen of the Zerg, then Zagara is the mother of the brood. Cunning and tenacious, she commands her minions on the field of battle. Encounter her brood and beware: Mess with her children, and you answer to mama.
infoText.Zarya: Aleksandra Zaryanova is one of the world's strongest women and was a promising athlete, but all that changed when the Siberian omnium reawakened. As a soldier in the Russian Defense Force, she now proudly uses her strength to protect the ones she loves.
infoText.Zeratul: The enigmatic dark templar Zeratul serves the xel'naga. While he expertly wields the power of the Void, it is his wisdom that earns him the respect of the Koprulu sector's leaders - despite his penchant for dramatic entrances.
infoText.Zuljin: Before the elves and their Alliance, the forests of Lordaeron belonged to the trolls of the Amani empire. As their warlord, Zul'jin has united the tribes into an army that stands ready to take back what is theirs and to slay the elves who stole it from them.

fullText.AbathurCombatStyleBombardStrain|AbathurL: Locust's Basic Attacks become a long-range siege attack and their duration is increased by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c>.
fullText.AbathurCombatStyleLocustBrood|AbathurLoc: Activate to spawn <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> Locusts at a nearby location.
fullText.AbathurCombatStyleSurvivalInstincts|Abat: Increases Locust's Health by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100%</c> and damage by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">60%</c>.
fullText.AbathurHeroicAbilityEvolveMonstrosity|Ab: Turn an allied Minion or Locust into a Monstrosity. When enemy Minions near the Monstrosity die, it gains <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2%</c> Health and <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2%</c> Basic Attack damage, stacking up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40</c> times. The Monstrosity can be healed by Carapace and has the ability to Burrow to a visible location every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">80</c> seconds.<n/><n/>Using Symbiote on the Monstrosity allows Abathur to control it, in addition to Symbiote's normal benefits. This Ability can be reactivated to automatically cast Symbiote on his Monstrosity.
fullText.AbathurHeroicAbilityUltimateEvolution|Ab: Clone target allied Hero and control it for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20</c> seconds. Abathur has perfected the clone, granting it <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c> Spell Power, <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c> bonus Attack Damage, and <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> bonus Movement Speed. Cannot use their Heroic Ability.
fullText.AbathurMasteryAdrenalineBoost|AbathurSym: Symbiote's Carapace increases the Movement Speed of the target by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3.5</c> seconds.
fullText.AbathurMasteryEnvenomedNestsToxicNest|Ab: Toxic Nests deal <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c> more damage over <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds and reduce the Armor of enemy Heroes hit by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds.
fullText.AbathurMasteryEnvenomedSpikes|AbathurSym: Abathur's Symbiote's Spike Burst also Slows enemy Movement Speed by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds.
fullText.AbathurMasteryEvolutionComplete|AbathurM: Increase Monstrosity's Basic Attack damage by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100%</c>.
fullText.AbathurMasteryLocustMaster|AbathurLocust: Activate to create a nest that periodically spawns Locusts. Only one Locust Nest can be active at a time.
fullText.AbathurMasteryNeedlespine|AbathurSymbiot: Increases the damage and range of Symbiote's Stab by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c>.
fullText.AbathurMasteryPressurizedGlands|AbathurS: Increases the range of Symbiote's Spike Burst by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">25%</c> and decreases the cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c> second.
fullText.AbathurMasteryProlificDispersal|AbathurT: Increase the duration of Toxic Nests by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">45</c> seconds, reduce its cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds, and add <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> additional charges.
fullText.AbathurMasterySpatialEfficiency|AbathurS: Symbiote's Stab gains <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c> additional charge and its cooldown is reduced by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">.5</c> seconds.
fullText.AbathurMasteryVileNestsToxicNest|Abathur: Toxic Nests Slow enemy Movement Speed by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2.5</c> seconds.
fullText.AbathurReinforcedCarapace|AbathurReinfor: Increase the Shield amount of Carapace by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c>.
fullText.AbathurSymbioteAdrenalOverload|AbathurSy: Heroic Symbiote hosts gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">25%</c> increased Attack Speed.
fullText.AbathurSymbioteCarapaceNetworkedCarapace: Using Symbiote's Carapace also applies an untalented Carapace Shield to all nearby allied Heroes, Minions, and Mercenaries.
fullText.AbathurSymbioteHivemind|AbathurSymbioteH: Symbiote creates an additional Symbiote on a nearby allied Hero. This Symbiote mimics the commands of the first, but does half damage and Shielding.
fullText.AbathurSymbioteSpikeBurstSomaTransferenc: Symbiote's Spike Burst heals the host for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">64</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> Health per enemy Hero hit.
fullText.AbathurUltimateEvolutionEvolutionaryLink: Increase the duration of Ultimate Evolution by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c>. As long as the Ultimate Evolution is alive, the original target of the clone gains a Shield equal to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">35%</c> of their maximum Health. Refreshes every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5</c> seconds.
fullText.AbathurVolatileMutation|AbathurVolatileM: Ultimate Evolution clones and Monstrosities deal <c val="#TooltipNumbers">137</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to nearby enemies every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds and when they die. Enemy Heroes hit heal the clone or Monstrosity for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100%</c> of the damage dealt.
fullText.AlarakAppliedForce|AlarakAppliedForce|W|: Reduce Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c>, but Telekinesis pushes <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> further and has <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> increased range.
fullText.AlarakBladeoftheHighlord|AlarakBladeofth: Basic Attacks against Heroes increase Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2%</c> stacking up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c>.<n/><n/>Sadism gained from Blade of the Highlord is lost on death.
fullText.AlarakChaosReigns|AlarakChaosReigns|Q|Le: Quest: Hit Heroes with Discord Strike.Reward: After hitting 15 Heroes, increase Discord Strike's damage by 60.Reward: After hitting 2 Heroes with a single Discord Strike, increase its damage by 60.Reward: After hitting 3 Heroes with a single Discord Strike, increase its damage by 100 and instantly gain all other Rewards.
fullText.AlarakDeadlyChargeFirstHeroic|AlarakDead: After channeling, Alarak charges forward dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">200</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to all enemies in his path. Distance is increased based on the amount of time channeled, up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1.5</c> seconds.<n/><n/>Issuing a Move order while this is channeling will cancel it at no cost. Taking damage will interrupt the channeling.
fullText.AlarakDissonance|AlarakDissonance|Q|Leve: Increase Discord Strike's Silence duration by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">0.75</c> seconds.
fullText.AlarakExtendedLightning|AlarakExtendedLi: Reduce Sadism by 10%.Quest: Hit Heroes with the center of Lightning Surge.Reward: After hitting 5 Heroes, increase Lightning Surge's range by 20%.Reward: After hitting 15 Heroes, Lightning Surge's center also Slows enemies by 40% for 2 seconds.Reward: After hitting 3 Heroes with the center of a single cast, increase Sadism by 10% and instantly gain all other Rewards.
fullText.AlarakHastyBargain|AlarakHastyBargain|Ac: Activate to permanently reduce Alarak's Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4%</c> and reset the cooldowns of his Basic Abilities.
fullText.AlarakHeroicAbilityCounterStrike|AlarakC: Alarak targets an area and channels for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c> second, becoming Protected and Unstoppable. After, if he took damage from an enemy Hero, he sends a shockwave that deals <c val="#TooltipNumbers">275</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage.
fullText.AlarakHinderedMotion|AlarakHinderedMotio: Telekinesis Slows enemies by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds.
fullText.AlarakLastLaugh|AlarakLastLaugh|Active|L: Activate to teleport to the chosen location and remove all Roots, Slows, and damage over time effects. If Alarak fails to hit enemy Heroes <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> times with Basic Abilities within <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds of using Last Laugh, his Health is reduced to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c>.
fullText.AlarakLethalOnslaughtDiscordStrike|Alara: Hitting an enemy Hero with Discord Strike grants <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c> of Sadism's damage bonus to Alarak's Basic Attacks for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds. Basic Attacks against Heroes refresh this duration.
fullText.AlarakLightningBarrage|AlarakLightningBa: Hitting an enemy Hero with the center of Lightning Surge allows it to be cast again for free on a different primary target within the next <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds. This free cast cannot benefit from Lightning Barrage.
fullText.AlarakMightOfTheHighlord|AlarakMightOfTh: Alarak gains access to both of his Heroic Abilities. This can be activated to change which Ability occupies the Heroic slot, with the other Ability using the Trait slot.
fullText.AlarakMockingStrikes|AlarakMockingStrike: Basic Attacks against enemy Heroes reduce the cooldown of Alarak's Basic Abilities by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1.5</c> seconds. This bonus is doubled against enemy Heroes who are Stunned, Silenced, Slowed, or Rooted.
fullText.AlarakNegativelyCharged|AlarakNegatively: Increase the Lightning Surge damage bonus to enemies between Alarak and his victim to 175%.Repeatable Quest: Each Hero hit by the center of Lightning Surge permanently increases the damage bonus by 5%.
fullText.AlarakOverwhelmingPowerDiscordStrike|Ala: Hitting an enemy Hero with Discord Strike grants <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> Attack Speed and causes Basic Attacks to heal for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> of the damage dealt, increased by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c> of Sadism's bonus when hitting Heroes. These effects last for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds.
fullText.AlarakPureMalice|AlarakPureMalice|Trait|: Increase Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> any time an allied Hero dies, up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c>.<n/><n/>Sadism gained from Pure Malice is lost on death.
fullText.AlarakRiteofRakShir|AlarakRiteofRakShir|: Activate to mark an enemy Hero for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">300</c> seconds.<n/><n/>Hitting the marked Hero with Discord Strike increases Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3%</c>. Killing the marked Hero increases Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5%</c> and lowers the cooldown of Rite of Rak'Shir to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> seconds.<n/><n/>Sadism gained from Rite of Rak'Shir is lost on death.
fullText.AlarakRuthlessMomentum|AlarakRuthlessMom: Reduce the Mana cost of Telekinesis from <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30</c> to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20.</c> Telekinesis's cooldown recharges <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100%</c> faster while above <c val="#TooltipNumbers">80%</c> Health.
fullText.AlarakShowofForce|AlarakShowofForce|Trai: Heroes hit with <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> of Alarak's Abilities within <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds take an additional <c val="#TooltipNumbers">61</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage and increase Sadism by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2%</c>, stacking up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c>. <n/><n/>Sadism gained from Show of Force is lost on death.
fullText.AlarakWrathOfTheHighlord|AlarakWrathOfTh: If Deadly Charge was the selected Heroic, Heroes hit by Deadly Charge take bonus damage from Alarak's Abilities equal to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c> of Sadism's bonus damage for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds.<n/><n/>If Counter-Strike was the selected Heroic, its cooldown is reduced by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2.5</c> seconds for each enemy Hero hit by Alarak's Basic Abilities.
fullText.AlexstraszaAncientFlameFlameBuffet|Alexs: While Dragonqueen is active, Alexstrasza's Basic Attacks apply Flame Buffet.
fullText.AlexstraszaBlessingOfTheRed|AlexstraszaB: Activate to grant an allied Hero <c val="#TooltipNumbers">500</c> bonus maximum Health until they die.<n/><n/>Cannot be used on Alexstrasza, or on a Hero who already has Blessing of the Red.
fullText.AlexstraszaCircleOfLifeAbundance|Alexstr: Quest: Collect Regeneration Globes.Reward: After collecting 10 Regeneration Globes, Abundance heals for an additional 5% maximum Health.Reward: After collecting 15 Regeneration Globes, Abundance's healing burst creates a Regeneration Globe.
fullText.AlexstraszaCleansingFlame|AlexstraszaCle: After <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1.25</c> seconds, take to the sky and drop <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5</c> fireballs over <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c> seconds at the position of the mouse cursor. Fireballs deal <c val="#TooltipNumbers">135</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to enemies and heal allied Heroes for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">300</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> Health.<n/><n/><c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds after dropping all fireballs, Alexstrasza lands at the position of the mouse cursor.
fullText.AlexstraszaDraconicDisciplineDragonqueen: Increase Dragonqueen's duration by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c> seconds, and Basic Attack healing by<c val="#TooltipNumbers"> 50%</c>.
fullText.AlexstraszaDragonScales|AlexstraszaDrago: Gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50</c> Armor while Stunned, Rooted, or Silenced, and for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds after. Can only trigger once every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> seconds.
fullText.AlexstraszaExuberance|AlexstraszaExubera: While above <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c> Health, gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> Movement Speed.
fullText.AlexstraszaFireWithinFlameBuffet|Alexstr: Hitting a Burning Hero with Flame Buffet heals Alexstrasza for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> of her maximum Health.
fullText.AlexstraszaFlamesOfFuryFlameBuffet|Alexs: Reduce the Mana cost of Flame Buffet from 50 to 40.Quest: Hit 20 Burning Heroes with Flame Buffet.Reward: Hitting a Burning Hero with Flame Buffet reduces Dragonqueen's cooldown by 5 seconds.
fullText.AlexstraszaHeatExhaustionFlameBuffet|Ale: Increase Flame Buffet's Slow from <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c> and cause it to no longer decay.
fullText.AlexstraszaLifebinder|AlexstraszaLifebin: Bind Alexstrasza's life force with an allied Hero. Both her and her target are healed for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">480</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> Health over <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds. Afterwards, the Hero with a lower percentage of Health is healed to the same Health percentage as the other Hero.<n/><n/><c val="#ColorViolet">Dragonqueen:</c> Cast range increased by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">45%</c>.
fullText.AlexstraszaLifeblossomGiftOfLife|Alexstr: While Alexstrasza is above <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c> Health, Gift of Life creates a Lifeblossom at the target's location. Alexstrasza can collect the Lifeblossom to make her next Gift of Life cost no Health.<n/><n/><c val="#AbilityPassive">Passive:</c> Reduces the cost of Gift of Life to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> of Alexstrasza's current Health.
fullText.AlexstraszaLifeUnbound|AlexstraszaLifeUn: Activate to heal an allied Hero for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> of their maximum Health and deal <c val="#TooltipNumbers">200</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to nearby enemies.<n/><n/>Healing a Stunned, Rooted, or Silenced ally reduces Life Unbound's cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30</c> seconds.<n/><n/><c val="ffff8a">Cannot be used on Alexstrasza.</c>
fullText.AlexstraszaLiveAndLetLiveGiftOfLife|Alex: While Alexstrasza is above <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c> Health, Gift of Life's cooldown recharges <c val="#TooltipNumbers">125%</c> faster.
fullText.AlexstraszaOnRubyWingsCleansingFlame|Ale: Healing or damaging Heroes <c val="#TooltipNumbers">8</c> or more times with Cleansing Flame activates Dragonqueen for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15</c> seconds upon landing.
fullText.AlexstraszaOverprotectiveAbundance|Alexs: Heroes healed by Abundance gain a Shield for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c> seconds equal to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">70%</c> of the amount healed.
fullText.AlexstraszaPacify|AlexstraszaPacify|Acti: Activate to Slow and reduce an enemy Hero's damage by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds.<n/><n/>Healing a Stunned, Rooted, or Silenced ally reduces Pacify's cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30</c> seconds.
fullText.AlexstraszaRitualOfLife|AlexstraszaRitua: Life-Binder activates <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> times over <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c> seconds.
fullText.AlexstraszaSurgeOfVitalityAbundance|Alex: Abundance's healing burst grants allied Heroes <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30%</c> Movement Speed for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds.
fullText.AlexstraszaToughLoveGiftOfLife|Alexstras: Gift of Life grants its target <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> Armor for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2.5</c> seconds, increased to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30</c> while Alexstrasza is above <c val="#TooltipNumbers">75%</c> Health.
fullText.AlexstraszaVerdantFlourishAbundance|Alex: Alexstrasza receives <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> more healing from Abundance and <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100%</c> more healing from Regeneration Globes.
fullText.Amazon-AvoidanceRockstopper|AmazonAvoida: Activate to fully charge Avoidance and increase its Armor value to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">60</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds.  <n/><n/>Losing Avoidance removes this bonus.
fullText.AmazonBallLightning|AmazonBallLightning|: Throw a ball of lightning at an enemy Hero that bounces up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c> times between nearby enemy Heroes and Cassia, dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">180</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to enemies hit.
fullText.AmazonChargedStrikes|AmazonAvoidanceChar: Gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> Attack Speed. Every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3rd</c> Basic Attack deals <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c> bonus damage and bounces to nearby enemy Heroes.
fullText.AmazonFendGlovesOfAlacrity|AmazonFendGlo: Gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c> Basic Attack range. While Avoidance is fully charged, gain an additional <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c> Basic Attack range.

fullText.AmazonImprisoningLight|AmazonValkyrieImp: Upon impaling an enemy Hero, they are Blinded for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5</c> seconds and Cassia's Valkyrie creates a wave of light that deals <c val="#TooltipNumbers">200</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to nearby enemies. fullText.AmazonInfiniteLightning|AmazonBallLightn: Ball Lightning can now bounce indefinitely. Every bounce reduces its cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds.
fullText.AmazonInnerLight|AmazonLightningFuryInne: Activate to heal for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">25%</c> of your maximum Health over <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5</c> seconds. Enemy Heroes hit with Lightning Fury lower the cooldown of this ability by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> seconds.
fullText.AmazonMartialLaw|AmazonMartialLaw|Passiv: Basic Attacks against enemy Heroes deal bonus damage equal to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1%</c> of their maximum Health. Increase this to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3%</c> if they are Stunned, Rooted, Slowed, or Blinded.
fullText.AmazonPlateoftheWhale|AmazonAvoidancePla: Regenerate <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> Health per second while Avoidance is active. fullText.AmazonPowerStrike|AmazonPowerStrike|Q|Le: Lightning Fury pierces but only splits on Heroes. fullText.AmazonRingoftheLeech|AmazonRingoftheLeec: Basic Attacks against your primary target heal for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c> of their damage. If an enemy is Blinded, this also affects Lightning Fury and Fend.

fullText.AmazonStaticElectricity|AmazonFendStatic: Gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> additional charges of Lightning Fury. Fend's impact launches a lightning bolt towards each nearby enemy Hero.
fullText.AmazonSurgeOfLight|AmazonAvoidanceSurgeO: After taking <c val="#TooltipNumbers">650</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage with Avoidance active, Cassia can activate Avoidance to deal <c val="#TooltipNumbers">220</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to enemies around her.
fullText.AmazonThunderstroke|AmazonLightningFuryT: Increase the damage of Lightning Fury by 20.Quest: Lightning Fury's damage is increased by 1.5 every time it hits a Hero.
fullText.AmazonTitansRevenge|AmazonTitansRevenge|: Cassia's Basic Attacks now ignore Armor and while Avoidance is fully charged, they deal an additional <c val="#TooltipNumbers">20%</c> bonus damage.
fullText.AmazonValkyrie|AmazonValkyrie|Heroic|Lev: Summon a Valkyrie that rushes to Cassia after <c val="#TooltipNumbers">0.75</c> seconds, pulling the first enemy Hero hit, dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">225</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage and Stunning them for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">0.5</c> seconds at the end of her path. fullText.AmazonWarTraveler|AmazonAvoidanceWarTrav: Gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5%</c> Movement Speed. While Avoidance is fully charged, gain an additional <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> Movement Speed and Mounting is instant.

fullText.AnaDynamicOptics|AnaDynamicOptics|Trait|: Basic Attacks increase Attack Speed by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds, up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100%</c>. fullText.AnaEyeOfHorusDeadeye|AnaEyeOfHorusDeadey: Eye of Horus cooldown reduced to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">6</c> seconds, but it only regains new rounds every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> seconds. fullText.AnaHealingDartSharpshooter|AnaHealingDar: Healing Dart's healing is increased by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5%</c> if it heals a Hero, up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c>. Allies are healed for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">225</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> and enemies are damaged for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">135</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c>. fullText.AnaHeroicAbilityNanaBoost|AnaNanoBoost|H: Instantly boost an allied Hero, restoring <c val="#TooltipNumbers">200</c> Mana. fullText.AnaSleepingDartSlumberShells|AnaSleeping: Reduce the cooldown of Sleep Dart by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds. Upon waking, enemy Heroes are Slowed by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">25%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds.

fullText.ChromieSandBlastOnceAgainTheFirstTime|Ch: Quest: Hit 40 Heroes with Sand Blast from Chromie or her Echoes.Reward: Increase the maximum number of active Echoes to 2, and increase Echo damage to 45%.
fullText.ChromieSandBlastPiercingSands|ChromieSan: Sand Blasts cast from Chromie and her Echoes now pierce all enemies hit.
fullText.ChromieSandBlastShiftingSands|ChromieSan: Hitting an enemy Hero with Chromie's primary Sand Blast grants <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5%</c> Spell Power for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">8</c> seconds, up to a maximum of <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c>. The duration is refreshed whenever an enemy is damaged by the primary Sand Blast.
fullText.ChromieSlowingSandsUnravelling|ChromieSl: Every <c val="#TooltipNumbers">0.25</c> seconds, enemy Heroes Slowed by Slowing Sands have their Spell Armor reduced by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10</c> up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50</c>.<n/><n/><c val="#AbilityPassive">Passive:</c> Increase Slowing Sands cast range by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c>, and Slowing Sands persists for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5</c> seconds after it is cancelled.
fullText.ChromieTemporalLoopStuckInALoop|ChromieT: Temporal Loop teleports the target a second time after the first, and Basic Abilities continue recharging for an additional <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds. fullText.ChromieTimeOut|ChromieTimeOutHotbar|Acti: Activate to place Chromie in Stasis and gain Invulnerability for up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">5</c> seconds. Can be reactivated to end the effect early.
fullText.ChromieTimeTrapAndorhalAnomaly|ChromieTi: Increase the charges and maximum active Time Traps to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c>, and reduce its cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">8</c> seconds.
fullText.ChromieTimeTrapChronicConditions|Chromie: After its Time Stop ends, enemies affected by Time Trap are Slowed by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30%</c> for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds and allies gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30%</c> Movement Speed for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds.
fullText.ChromieTimeTrapTimeTroubles|ChromieTimeT: After its Time Stop ends, enemies affected by Time Trap lose <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15</c> Armor for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds and allies gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">30</c> Armor for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> seconds.
fullText.ChromieTimewalkersPursuit|ChromieTimewal: Gain <c val="#TooltipNumbers">15%</c> Spell Power and reduce the arming time of Time Traps by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">0.75</c> seconds. fullText.CrusaderBlessedHammer|CrusaderCondemnBle: Activate to create <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> hammers that spiral outward from Johanna, dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">65</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to enemies hit. fullText.CrusaderBlessedMomentum|JohannaBlessedMo: Basic Attacks grant <c val="#TooltipNumbers">10%</c> increased Attack Speed for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds, stacking up to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c>. fullText.CrusaderIronSkinSteedCharge|CrusaderIron: Activate to instantly Mount for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds, unhindered by damage.<n/><n/><c val="#AbilityPassive">Passive:</c> Increase the duration of Iron Skin by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> second.
fullText.CrusaderMasteryBlessedShieldRadiatingFai: Increase the number of enemies hit by Blessed Shield by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c>. Its Stun duration is increased to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1.75</c> seconds and all targets hit are Stunned for the maximum duration.

fullText.CrusaderMasteryFallingSwordHeavensFury|C: Falling Sword rains down holy bolts, dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage to enemies, healing allied Heroes for <c val="#TooltipNumbers">68</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c>, and reducing its cooldown by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">1</c> second for each Hero hit.
fullText.CrusaderMasteryIronSkinHoldYourGround|Cr: Increases Iron Skin's Shield by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">40%</c> and its cooldown is reduced by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> seconds.

fullText.CrusaderMasteryPunishRoar|CrusaderRoarTa: Increase Punish's damage by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">50%</c>. This bonus is increased to <c val="#TooltipNumbers">150%</c> whenever Punish hits <c val="#TooltipNumbers">2</c> or more Heroes.

fullText.DeathwingConflagration|DeathwingConflagr: Dealing <c val="#TooltipNumbers">320</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage with Molten Flame to enemy Heroes within <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds causes them to burn for an additional <c val="#TooltipNumbers">100</c><c val="#ColorGray"> (+4% per level)</c> damage over <c val="#TooltipNumbers">4</c> seconds. fullText.DeathwingDragonsIre|DeathwingDragonsIre|: While Deathwing has <c val="#TooltipNumbers">3</c> or fewer Armor Plates, his damage is increased by <c val="#TooltipNumbers">25%</c>.
